stages.name: public_docs_doctest
stages.evidence: test/public_docs_doctest_test runs the selected public contract docs and the test/fixtures/doctest/public_docs coverage fixtures through the doctest engine (zentinel.doctest_command.run) with a deterministic injected executor: docs/CLI_SPEC.md yields a passing CLI case (zentinel version), docs/CONFIG_SPEC.md a passing config case, docs/REPORT_FORMAT.md a passing report JSON case validated as a json-subset of zentinel.report.v1, and docs/DOCTEST_AI_INTEGRATION.md a passing doctest AI JSON case validated as a json-subset of zentinel.ai.doctest.suggest.response.v1. A verifier-artifact fixture asserts that the verifier references doctest evidence via a public_docs_doctest stage.

stages.name: unit_tests
stages.evidence: full suite green (469 tests); the new test/public_docs_doctest_test adds 9 cases and no existing doctest snapshot or report test regressed.

stages.name: mutation_checks
stages.reason: task 066 adds doctest coverage (a new test, doc examples, and JSON fixtures) without changing mutator or deterministic-core decision code; per the non-goals it does not add mutation-aware doctest gating for every public doc. The final release dogfood gate is task 085.

residual_risk: Public-docs JSON examples are validated as supported subsets (json expected subset) of the documented schema_version and key fields, not as full JSON Schema validation; this keeps the examples small and deterministic while still catching schema_version or envelope drift.

The doctest engine executes the documented commands through an injected executor; the coverage tests use deterministic canned outputs (as the existing CLI doctest tests do), so they assert documentation executability and subset matching rather than a full end-to-end run of zentinel run / doctest suggest.

Only the selected public contract docs are covered (CLI, config, report, doctest AI); broadening coverage to every public doc and mutation-aware doctest gating are explicit non-goals deferred to later tasks.
